title: Public Showers/Baths - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Public Showers/Baths 73 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Women Homeless Youth AGAPE FELLOWSHIP HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers showers. 1089 SW 50th Street Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Sunday noon-until capacity is reached (541) 994-3166 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AGAPE HOUSE ESSENTIAL NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, blankets, and household goods depending on availability. Shower facilities also available. Donations accepted. 500 West Harper Road Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm, Friday 9am-noon Showers: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-11:30am and 1pm-2:30pm, Friday 9am-11:30am Email (541) 567-8774 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASTORIA RESCUE MISSION COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves three community meals including breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Operates a clothing closet, showers, and a laundry facility allowing one load per week. Accepts donations of hygiene products such as shampoo, conditioner, body wash, toilet paper, and toothpaste. 62 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Breakfast: 7 days per week 7am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Showers: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Laundry: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Clothing Closet: 7 days per week 11am-5pm Email (503) 325-6243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLUB HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a recovery drop-in center with support services for substance addiction, including support groups and an employment center. Additional services include laundry facilities, showers, and snacks. Services are available for individuals at any stage of recovery. 100 SE Cleveland Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Drop-In Center: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Showers and laundry: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm (503) 933-2440 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT TEEN DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers a food pantry, help applying for state benefits such as OHP and SNAP, showers, laundry facilities. Also offers assistance with educational needs such as re-enrollment, school supplies, and specialized information and referral. Eligibility: Ages 11-24 that are homeless and unaccompanied by an adult Hours: Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm Email (800) 660-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides drop-in services including information and referral, showers, laundry facilities, a community meal, snacks, and coffee. Offers access to a phone, community voicemail, mailing address, and computers. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Non-parenting women age 25 and older who are experiencing homelessness. Well-mannered pets are allowed. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Lunch: 11:30am-1pm Email (971) 222-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A collaborative project that provides homeless families priority access to a coordinated set of services offered at multiple locations in the G Street neighborhood. The participating organizations function as a campus, with all services within easy walking distance. Services include: -A safe place to be during the day -Access to laundry, showers, and restroom facilities -A place to prepare and eat simple meals -Help finding affordable housing and employment -Access to additional school and community based resources 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness and at least one child age 17 and younger in their care Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE LAUNDRY AND SHOWER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shower and laundry services. Individuals may do 2 loads of laundry per week. Dryer access is limited to 30 minutes per load. Services are available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 231 Lyon Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHURCH @ THE PARK SAFE PARKING C@P SERVICE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a place for people to take showers, do laundry, access case management services, and be indoors. 2640 Portland Road NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (971) 375-7491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER SHOWERS AND LAUNDRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry facilities and hot showers with shampoo, soap, and a towel. Change of underclothes provided when available. Showers limited to 15 minutes. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Showers: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm Laundry: Tuesday-Thursday 1pm-4pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION SHOWER CART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mobile shower cart at two weekly locations. 364 9th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 12:30pm-3:30pm at 400 block of Avenue U (across from golf course) Tuesday 9am-12:30pm at Clatsop Community Action Office (364 9th Street, Astoria, OR) Email (503) 325-1400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management, transitional housing, rapid re-housing, permanent supportive housing, and veteran's housing services. Offers personal grooming items and shower vouchers when available. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness or at risk of Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM ASSISTANCE FOR PEOPLE WITHOUT HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry, showers, propane, tarps, and blankets. Also provides sleeping bags when available. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as homeless Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth drop-in center. Services include job finding assistance, resume and cover letter support, interview practice, career mentorship, assistance with completing college and scholarship applications, information and referral to housing and healthcare community resources, and access to shower and laundry facilities. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Wednesday-Thursday 3pm-6pm, Friday varies by month Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORE CORE SHOWER SERVICES SHOWER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to showers. 1300 Mckenzie Highway Sisters, OR 97759 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Saturday 6pm-7:30pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INIATIVES AT MIDLAND LIBRARY MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 805 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CI HYGIENE HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides showers, laundry services, clothing, and hygiene products. 14625 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Friday 10:30am-3pm, Tuesday-Thursday 9:30am-2pm Email (503) 477-9742 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT ALL SAINTS EPISCOPAL CHURCH MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 4033 SE Woodstock Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Saturday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT EASTSIDE IMAGO DEI COMMUNITY MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 3130 SE 148th Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT FAMILY OF GRACE MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 12414 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT FREEDOM FOURSQUARE CHURCH MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 660 SE 160th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 11am-2pm Holiday hours and unexpected closures can be found on www.instagram.com/cultivateinitiatives Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT LENTS WELLNESS FAIR MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 4244 SE 91st Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT MONTAVILLA WELLNESS FAIR MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 247 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT WESHINE VILLAGE MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 12505 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email (503) 477-9742 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV DAY CENTER HIV SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center serving hot breakfast and lunches, access to phones and computers, and recreational activities such as art therapy, games, and video-based media. Also provides clothing, showers, mail services, and hygiene supplies. Guests have access to laundry services, massages, acupuncture, foot care, and haircuts on a sign-up basis. 2941 NE Ainsworth Street Basement Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Individuals who are HIV-positive and have incomes no greater than 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday 1pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 460-3822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS COALITION COMMUNITY SOCIAL HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 2100 social services, nutritional hot meals, health behavior education, essential clothing, laundry and shower vouchers, temporary PO Box, and pet food, and resource navigation, tobacco, overdose, and suicide services. 4590 Florence Highway 101 N Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: People eligible under SNAP food assistance program Hours: Community Social Health Center: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 11am-1pm Hot meal: Monday/Wednesday/Friday noon-1pm Email (541) 997-5057 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 494 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Thursday 6pm-8pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details HYGIENE4ALL HYGIENE4ALL HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a community hygiene, sanitation, and safety hub. Provides showers, bedding in exchange for soiled, a clothing swap, menstruation and moontime hygiene supplies, first-aid kits, bathrooms, legal disposal for household trash and needles, foot, wound, and mouth care, and a warm outdoor space with coffee and tea. Also offers paid work and opportunities for advocacy. 824 SE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 3pm-8:30pm Open regardless of weather. Last shower 45 minutes before close. Search for Hygiene4all Hub on Google Maps for unanticipated holiday or other closures. Email (971) 236-2524 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY FAMILY COALITION BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an emergency food pantry, clothing vouchers, laundry vouchers, shower vouchers, a hygiene closet, assistance with identification cards and birth certificates, and bus passes with transportation to job interviews. 535 E River Street Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 592-6139 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term overnight shelter, two meals per day, and cleanup facilities for 30 homeless and street dependent youth attempting to make changes in their lives. Youth may access clothing, medical assistance, and employment education support. When severe weather is declared by the city, 5 additional beds will open. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Displaced youth ages 16-24. Youth must be working with a case manager on a plan in order to access a bed on a continuing basis. Hours: 7 days per week 9pm-9am Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK MADRAS SHELTER SERVICE CENTER MADRAS SHELTER DAYTIME SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center, meals, case management, showers, and laundry services. Volunteer opportunities available. 61 NW Oak Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted with a focus on individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Drop in center: 7 days per week 7am-1pm and 6pm-10pm Lunch: 7 days per week 11am-12:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 6pm-8pm Showers: Monday/Friday 7:30am-12:30pm Laundry: Thursdays 7am-1pm drop off, Friday pick up times vary Email (458) 800-4208 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN PDX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center. Services include access to showers, bathrooms, mail pick up and PO Box setup, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, on-site WiFi and telephone usage, and specialized information and referral. 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted with priority given to homeless individuals or families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (503) 232-2031 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER HOMELESS DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A day center that provides meals, a place to rest and socialize, housing resource materials, and WiFi. On-site housing liaison is available during the week. Mental health services are sometimes available on-site through partner agency. 12264 SW Hall Boulevard Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Day center hours: 7 days per week 7am-7pm -Breakfast 9am-10:15am -Lunch noon-1pm -Social Services 1pm-2pm -Showers by appointment only Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES DAYTIME NAVIGATION SHELTER LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center with access to socks, laundry facilities, Wi-Fi, computer and telephone access, fax, coffee, snacks, a clothing closet, assistance in navigating social services, and specialized information and referral. Camping gear may be available, depending on donations. 1076 Franklin Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 6am-6pm, Thursday/Friday 8am-6pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with laundry facilities, phone use, temporary mailing address, showers, and daily meals. Also provides street outreach, case management, mental health counseling, education, and assistance finding housing. Eligible youth will be added to a central housing referral list for all programs they qualify for. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: -Drop-in Center: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm -Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am-9:30am -Lunch: 7 days per week noon-1pm -Snack: 7 days per week 3pm-3:30pm -Dinner: 7 days per week 4pm-5pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVEONE COMMUNITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY BRAX LAUNDRY OREGON CITY LOVEONE MOBILE SHOWERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mobile shower units. Personal hygiene items available on a first come, first serve basis. 927 Molalla Avenue Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 4th Wednesday of the month 6pm-8pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE GLORIA CENTER MONTHLY DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to showers and laundry facilities, hygiene items, a hot meal, warming and cooling supplies (blankets, tarps, hats, gloves, socks, hand and toe warmers, hydration packets, cooling towels, sunscreen, and lip balm), and connections to community resources. 2505 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: January-October Last Friday of the month 9am-3pm, hot meal noon-3pm November-December 3rd Friday of the month 9am-3pm, hot meal noon-3pm Email (541) 298-5131 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY ARCHES PROJECT ARCHES DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in day center with services including showers, laundry, hygiene kits, meals, mailboxes, and resource navigation. 615 Commercial Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Day center and meals: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-4pm, breakfast 9am-10am, lunch noon-1pm Mail pick up: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Email (503) 399-9080 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOUNTAIN VIEW COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT SAFE PARKING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides safe parking facilities for community members living out of their car or a RV, and also has shower and laundry facilities. Partners with two local low-income housing complexes to provide case management support for placement into permanent housing. 1475 SW 35th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Permanent housing: age 55 and older or families with children Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 527-0028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier day center that offers computers and charging stations, a temporary mailing address, laundry facilities, restrooms, showers, and resources. Access to the day center is in four, 3-hour time blocks. To enter, participants need a ticket, and 25 tickets are distributed for each time block on a first-come, first-served basis. No ticket is required to pick up mail, which is available up to four times per day. Do Good Multnomah runs shelter and bridge housing programs on site to help people continue on their path to permanent housing. Referrals come through the Day Center. 333 SW Park Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, pets allowed Hours: Day Center: 7 days per week 8am-8pm (intake stops at 7:45pm), closed the 4th Friday of the month from noon-3pm Mail pick up: 9am-10am, noon-1pm, 3pm-4pm, 6pm-7pm Time Block 1: 8am-10:45am Time Block 2: 11am-1:45pm Time Block 3: 2pm-4:45pm Time Block 4: 5pm-7:45pm Email (503) 988-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER ALBA COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term emergency shelter, mentorship, family mediation, resource referrals, and support with basic needs, including food, clothing, showers, and hygiene supplies with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 9-17 and experiencing housing instability Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 754-4350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Services include information and referral, laundry, clothing, showers, lunch, mailbox access, mental health support groups, and peer support services. Also connects clients with medical services at West Salem Medical Clinic. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 588-5827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHRA OHRA COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing services, case management, information and referral, mailing address, telephone, benefits and services assistance, job search-related printing, resume creation, hygiene supplies, and education opportunities. Showers and laundry are available twice a week. Each individual who showers can do a small load of laundry. 2350 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers and Laundry: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm. First come, first serve. Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPEN DOOR HOUSINGWORKS UNHOUSED DAYTIME ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with access to hot meals, food boxes, showers, personal hygiene items, telephones, mail services, and case management support. 34420 SW Tualatin Valley Highway Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Food Boxes: Unrestricted Homeless services: Income must be at or below 50% Area Medan Income (AMI) Other services: Individuals experiencing or at-risk of homelessness Hours: Day center services: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm Hot meals: Monday-Friday noon-1pm Food boxes: Monday-Friday 9am-2:30pm Email (503) 640-6689 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A drop in center that provides basic needs services such as meals, recreation/activities, showers, laundry, phone, mailing address, and computers. Also offers a safe space for LGBTQ youth. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are experiencing homelessness and are eligible to enter the Homeless Youth Continuum Hours: Drop-In: Tuesday/Thursday/Sunday 1pm-6pm Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10am Lunch: Sunday-Friday 1pm-2pm Dinner: Sunday-Friday 5pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, showers, laundry, temporary mailing addresses, clothing provisions, cell phone charging, TriMet tickets, shelter and housing referrals, and connections to various community partners and services. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals who are homeless Hours: Center: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Meals: Breakfast 9am, sack lunches noon, hot meal 3pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center offering lunch, food boxes, laundry, showers, computers with internet access, assistance with housing applications and cover letters, and temporary mailing addresses.
